questions.question: What is the biggest mealtime challenge in your home?
options: My helper and I aren't aligned
My child only eats a few things
questions.presenterNote: We asked you this as you arrived. Here's what you said.

questions.question: Children must finish everything on their plate.
questions.answerNote: Forcing children to finish teaches them to ignore their own fullness signals. A child who learns to stop when full builds a healthier relationship with food for life.

questions.question: Fruit makes the body 'cold' and should be avoided.
questions.answerNote: This is a common belief in Chinese culture but has no clinical basis. Fruit is essential for vitamins, fibre, and hydration. Serve at room temperature if the family prefers — but don't avoid it.

questions.question: If a child refuses a food once, they don't like it.
questions.answerNote: It takes 10–15 exposures before a child accepts a new food. Refusing is not rejecting. Keep offering without pressure — let them see it, touch it, smell it first.

questions.question: Juice is just as good as whole fruit.
questions.answerNote: Whole fruit has fibre which slows sugar absorption. A glass of orange juice has almost the same sugar as a can of soda. Offer whole fruit and water instead.

questions.question: What is ONE thing you'll do differently — starting tomorrow?
